data.user_prompt: Recommend non-hospital mental health counseling options in Louisville, KY.
data.system_message: Answer as a local-search assistant for a person in Louisville, Kentucky. Prefer current local providers and cite source URLs when available.
data.tag: lmhg llm response perplexity Recommend non-hospital mental health counseling options in Louisville, KY.

sections.text:
For non-hospital mental health counseling in Louisville, KY, top options include **Kentucky Counseling Center** (offering in-person and telehealth services for children and adults), **Louisville Mental Health Group** (a private practice at 4229 Bardstown Rd providing individual, family, and group therapy), and **Kentucky Mental Health Care** (located at 645 Roy Wilkins Ave with therapists and IOPs) [1][2][4].

Here is a breakdown of recommended providers by service type:

| Provider | Key Services | Location/Format | Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Kentucky Counseling Center** | Counseling, psychiatry, medication management, IOPs, LGBTQ & family therapy | In-person (40217) & Telehealth (KCC Direct) | Affordable, compassionate, no long wait times [1] |
| **Louisville Mental Health Group** | Individual/family therapy, group therapy, EMDR, case management, reunification | In-person (4229 Bardstown Rd, Suite 310) | Welcoming new clients; multidisciplinary team [2] |
| **Kentucky Mental Health Care** | Therapy, counseling, child/addiction psychologists, IOPs | In-person (645 Roy Wilkins Ave) & Telehealth | Numerous clinicians; phone sessions available if no internet [4] |
| **Norton Healthcare** | Therapists, psychologists, psychiatrists, addiction specialists | Virtual & In-person (select locations) | Integrated team treating depression, anxiety, PTSD, eating disorders [5] |
| **Sage Mental Health** | Medication management, individual therapy for anxiety, ADHD, depression, PTSD | In-person & Telehealth | Welcomes all ages/backgrounds [8] |
| **University of Louisville (UofL)** | Confidential psychiatric services, counseling, group therapy | In-person (HSC & Cardinal Station) | **Free** for enrolled students; voluntary access [6] |
| **Acadia Counseling** | Mood/trauma therapy (depression, anxiety, dysphoria) | In-person (502-825-1375) | Opened 2019; serves adults, couples, teens [3] |
| **Renew Counseling Services** | Art/Expressive Therapy, EMDR, 40+ professionals | 4 locations in Louisville/Oldham | Large group practice with diverse therapy options [3] |

**Additional Resources:**
*   **Support Groups:** Psychology Today lists local groups for OCD, anxiety, and substance abuse recovery in Louisville [3].
*   **Immediate Care:** For urgent non-hospital mental health needs, **The Couch Immediate Mental Health Care** and **Access Behavioral Health** are highly reviewed options in the area [9].
*   **Peace Hospital Assessment:** If you or someone you love has depression symptoms, Peace Hospital offers a no-charge assessment and treatment assistance [7].

All listed providers offer outpatient, community-based care distinct from inpatient hospitalization. For the most current availability, contact them directly via their listed phone numbers or websites.
